title: Composition and method related to the recovery of germanium and rare earth elements
abstract: Disclosed herein is a method involving the steps of: a) mixing a first pond coal fly ash and a first ionic liquid, thereby leaching germanium from the first pond coal fly ash and producing a second pond coal fly ash and a first mixture comprising the first ionic liquid and the leached germanium: fa) separating the first mixture from the second pond coal fly ash; and c) mixing a first brine with the second pond coal fly ash, thereby leaching iron and one or more rare earth elements from the second pond coal fly ash and producing a third pond coal fly ash and a second mixture comprising the first brine and the leached iron and one or more rare earth elements.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising the steps of: a) mixing a first pond coal fly ash and a first ionic liquid, thereby leaching germanium from the first pond coal fly ash and producing a second pond coal fly ash and a first mixture comprising the first ionic liquid and the leached germanium; b) separating the first mixture from the second pond coal fly ash; and c) mixing a first brine with the second pond coal fly ash, thereby leaching iron and one or more rare earth elements from the second pond coal fly ash and producing a third pond coal fly ash and a second mixture comprising the first brine and the leached iron and one or more rare earth elements. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein method further comprises recovering the extracted germanium , 3. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein method further comprises regenerating and recycling the first ionic liquid. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the method further comprises adding an iron precipitating agent to the second mixture, thereby precipitating and removing at least a portion of the iron from the second mixture. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the method further comprises, after the step of adding the iron precipitating agent, mixing a second ionic liquid and the second mixture, thereby extracting the leached one or more rare earth elements from the second mixture. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the method further comprises recovering the extracted one or more rare earth elements. 7. The method of claims 5 or 6, wherein method further comprises regenerating and recycling the second ionic liquid. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the first ionic liquid comprises an anionic group selected from the group consisting of a phosphonic acid group, a phosphate group, and a phosphonate group. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the first ionic liquid comprises an imidazole derivative as a cationic group. 10. The method of any one of claims 5-7, wherein the second ionic liquid comprises an anionic group selected from ihe group consisting of a phosphonic acid group, a phosphate group, and a phosphonate group. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the first brine comprises a flue gas desulfunzation brine. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the flue gas desulfunzation brine has a chloride concentration of at least 80,000 mg L, 13. The method of any one of claims 4-12, wherein the iron precipitating agent is an agent that increases the pH of the second mixture, thereby precipitating the iron in the form of iron hydroxide. Potential chemicals utilized in the process could include NaOH, Ca(OH) 2 , or other alkaline agents. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the one or more rare earth elements is selected from the group consisting of yttrium, praseodymium, neodymium, europium, and dysprosium, or a combination thereof. 15. Ihe method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the method is performed within the geographical borders of an ash producing plan site. 16. A composition comprising a first pond coal fly ash and a first ionic liquid. 17. Ihe composition of claim 16, wherein the first ionic liquid comprises an anionic group selected from the group consisting of a phosphonic acid group, a phosphate group, and a phosphonate group. 18. The composition of claim 16, wherein the first ionic liquid comprises an imidazole derivative as a cationic group. 19. A composition comprising a second pond coal fly ash and a first brine. 20. The composition of claim 19, wherein the first brine is a flue gas desuifurization brine. 21. The composition of claims 20 or 21, wherein the flue gas desuifurization brine has a chloride concentration of at least 80,000 mg/L. 22. A composition comprising a second ionic liquid and a second mixture comprising a first brine and one or more rare earth elements. 23. The composition of claim 22, wherein second ionic liquid comprises an anionic group selected from the group consisting of a phosphonic acid group, a phosphate group, and a phosphonate group. 24. Tire composition of claim 23, wherem the second ionic liquid comprises an imidazole derivative as a cationic group. 25. The composition of any one of claims 22-24, wherein the one or more rare earth elements is selected from the group consisting of yttrium, praseodymium, neodymium, europium, and dysprosium, or a combination thereof. 26. The composition of any one of claims 22-25, wherein the first brine is a flue gas desuifurization brine. 27. The composition of claim 26, w herein the flue gas desuifurization brine has a chloride concentration of at least 80,000 mg/L.
